metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that the skip-reason keyword vocabulary covers ALL credential-gated skip reasons in `tests/external/`; the current set (`cred`/`acli`/`live`/`api.token`) matches only Jira-gated skips and misses every LLM-gated skip reason (`ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, `agents extra`, `OPENAI_API_KEY`, `LANGFUSE_HOST`) — expand the vocabulary or switch to a marker-based detection strategy.
findings.finding: The plan's skip-reason keyword vocabulary (`cred`/`acli`/`live`/`api.token`) provably fails to match the actual skip messages used by LLM-credential-gated external tests. Reading every `_skip` definition across `tests/external/`: LLM tests use `"no ANTHROPIC_API_KEY / agents extra"`, `"needs ANTHROPIC_API_KEY"`, and `"needs an LLM key + agents extra + tracing extra + LANGFUSE_HOST/PUBLIC_KEY/SECRET_KEY"` — none of which contain `cred`, `acli`, `live`, or `api.token`. The keyword `api.token` matches zero skip messages in the entire repo. So the hook's condition (a) would never fire for an all-LLM-skip scenario, making the canary inoperative for the LLM tier.
findings.location: What § item 2 — fail-on-all-skip hook; AC line 2
findings.suggested_fix: Add keywords `anthropic`, `openai`, `api_key`, `agents` to the matching vocabulary, OR (more robustly) have each live test apply a custom pytest marker (e.g. `@pytest.mark.live_prerequisite`) and key the hook off that marker rather than skip-reason text.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the plan's claim that `test_external_tier_allows_model_requests` 'never skips': it calls `pytest.importorskip("pydantic_ai.models")` and will skip in lean (no `[agents]`) lanes; choose a genuinely unconditional guard (e.g. `test_deps_live.py` tests, which have no skipif and no importorskip) or note this conditionality in the hook's exclusion logic.
findings.finding: The plan asserts `test_external_tier_allows_model_requests` 'needs no creds and never skips' and uses it as the canonical example of an always-on guard test whose execution cannot mask an all-skip live tier. In reality, the test body calls `pytest.importorskip("pydantic_ai.models")` at line 19, which issues a SKIP (not a failure) when the `[agents]` extra is absent (lean lane). The plan's exclusion logic rests on this test being unconditionally executed, but it can itself skip.
findings.location: What § item 2 — always-on guard test exclusion; AC line 2
findings.suggested_fix: Use `test_deps_live.py::test_live_real_packages_refute` (no credentials, no importorskip, no skipif) as the canonical always-on example, or define the 'always-on' category by the absence of a credential-skip marker rather than by a named test.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the hook's skip-reason keyword set to cover actual LLM-credential skip reasons: add `anthropic`, `openai`, `api_key`, and `agents` (or the substring `key`) alongside `cred`/`acli`/`live`/`api.token`, so that 'no ANTHROPIC_API_KEY / agents extra', 'needs ANTHROPIC_API_KEY', and the Langfuse trace skip reason are all recognized as credential-gated skips.
findings.finding: The canary hook's skip-reason keyword set (`cred`/`acli`/`live`/`api.token`) does not match the actual skip reason strings used by LLM-gated external tests. Specifically, 'no ANTHROPIC_API_KEY / agents extra' (used by test_workflow_live, test_review_kernel_rules_live, test_pydantic_ai_cutover_live), 'needs ANTHROPIC_API_KEY' (test_execution_mode_live), and 'needs an LLM key + agents extra + tracing extra + LANGFUSE_HOST/PUBLIC_KEY/SECRET_KEY' (test_llm_trace) contain none of the four patterns. If LLM credentials are absent but Jira credentials ARE present, LLM-gated tests skip with unrecognized reasons — the hook may not count them as 'credential-gated skips', so condition (a) may fail to fire for an all-skip LLM tier, defeating the canary for that class of tests.
findings.location: What § 2 (conftest hook) — 'reason matching, case-insensitively, `cred`/`acli`/`live`/`api.token`'
findings.suggested_fix: Match on any of: `cred`, `acli`, `live`, `api.token`, `api_key`, `anthropic`, `openai`, `agents extra`, `langfuse` (case-insensitive). Alternatively, invert the approach: mark always-on tests with a custom marker (e.g. `pytest.mark.always_on`) and treat ALL other skips in the external tier as credential-gated, which is more robust to future skip reason wording changes.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify the skip-reason keyword vocabulary against every `reason=` string in `tests/external/` and either expand the vocabulary to cover LLM-gated skips (e.g. add `key`/`anthropic`/`openai`/`agent`) or document that LLM-tier skip detection is explicitly out of scope.
findings.finding: The plan's skip-reason keyword vocabulary (`cred`/`acli`/`live`/`api.token`) is asserted to capture 'missing live prerequisite' skips, but is unverified against the actual skip messages used in tests/external/. Three LLM-gated tests use skip reasons that match NONE of these keywords: `"no ANTHROPIC_API_KEY / agents extra"` (test_workflow_live.py:41, test_review_kernel_rules_live.py:48, test_pydantic_ai_cutover_live.py:45), `"needs ANTHROPIC_API_KEY"` (test_execution_mode_live.py:25), and `"needs an LLM key + agents extra + tracing extra + LANGFUSE_HOST/PUBLIC_KEY/SECRET_KEY"` (test_llm_trace.py:80–83). In a scheduled run where LLM keys are absent but Jira creds are present, these tests skip with unmatched reasons, so condition (a) of the hook's failure logic never fires for the LLM tier — LLM-tier all-skip goes undetected.
findings.location: What § 2 (conftest fail-on-all-skip hook) / AC line 2 — skip-reason keyword vocabulary
findings.suggested_fix: Add `key` and `anthropic` (or `openai`) to the matching vocabulary so `"no ANTHROPIC_API_KEY / agents extra"` and `"needs ANTHROPIC_API_KEY"` are classified as missing-live-prerequisite skips. Alternatively, define the match as 'any skip reason that does NOT contain "inert by default"' (i.e. treat only the explicit conftest opt-in skip as non-credential-gated), which covers all current and future credential-skip patterns without a keyword list.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Expand the skip-reason keyword vocabulary to cover all real live-prerequisite skip messages, including `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, `agents extra`, `LLM key`, and `LANGFUSE_*` patterns — or switch to matching any skip reason that is NOT the opt-in guard message (the `_require_external_opt_in` skip from conftest.py is the only non-credential skip baseline).
findings.finding: The skip-reason keyword vocabulary (`cred`/`acli`/`live`/`api.token`) defined for condition (a) of the fail-on-all-skip hook provably fails to match multiple real live-prerequisite skip messages used in the codebase: `"no ANTHROPIC_API_KEY / agents extra"` (test_workflow_live.py, test_review_kernel_rules_live.py, test_pydantic_ai_cutover_live.py), `"needs ANTHROPIC_API_KEY"` (test_execution_mode_live.py), and `"needs an LLM key + agents extra + tracing extra + LANGFUSE_HOST/PUBLIC_KEY/SECRET_KEY"` (test_llm_trace.py) contain none of the four tokens case-insensitively. This means real credential-gated skips will go undetected by condition (a), defeating the canary's detection logic for the LLM-tier tests.
findings.location: What, item 2 — conftest hook condition (a); tests/external/test_workflow_live.py:41; tests/external/test_execution_mode_live.py:25; tests/external/test_pydantic_ai_cutover_live.py:45; tests/external/test_llm_trace.py:80
findings.suggested_fix: Instead of a keyword allowlist for condition (a), invert the logic: treat any skip reason that is NOT the fixed opt-in guard message (`'external tests are inert by default'`) as a credential-gated skip. This is more robust and requires no vocabulary maintenance as new live tests are added.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either (a) harden the mirrored acli install step to `exit 1` when `ACLI_SHA256` is unset (making integrity verification mandatory for the scheduled run, not just a warning), or (b) explicitly acknowledge in the plan that SHA256 is advisory and explain why that is acceptable for this context.
findings.finding: The plan describes the acli download-verify-install step as 'sha256-checked' and instructs mirroring reconcile-bridge.yml's step 'verbatim in shape' — but reconcile-bridge.yml's actual SHA256 check is a soft warning (not a hard failure) when `ACLI_SHA256` is unset. If an operator configures `ACLI_VERSION` but omits `ACLI_SHA256` (a realistic error given AC item 3 lists them as a pair with no enforcement), the scheduled workflow downloads and executes an unverified binary from an external CDN on a runner that simultaneously holds `JIRA_API_TOKEN`, `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, and `OPENAI_API_KEY`. The plan's 'sha256-checked' characterization misrepresents the actual control: integrity verification is optional/advisory, not enforced.
findings.location: What §1 ('the Download + verify + install acli step (pinned vars.ACLI_VERSION + vars.ACLI_SHA256, sha256-checked)') / reconcile-bridge.yml lines 156–159
findings.suggested_fix: In What §1, change the instruction for the Download+verify step to: 'if ACLI_SHA256 is unset or empty, the step must exit 1 (not merely warn) — the scheduled canary run is a security-sensitive context because the runner holds multiple live API secrets.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the 'Why' section cleanup description: the live test does NOT call `delete_issue_link` (that command hangs per bug d843); it deletes only the issues (which removes links as a Jira side effect) — update to 'deletes each created issue (which cascades to remove its links)' to match the actual `try/finally` code.
findings.finding: The plan's 'Why' section asserts the live test 'deletes every artifact it created (links then issues).' The actual cleanup code in `test_link_sync_roundtrip_live.py` lines 418-431 does NOT call `delete_issue_link` — the code comment explicitly states the command 'currently hangs (no subprocess timeout — bug d843)' and deletes only the issues (which also removes links as a side effect). The plan's description 'links then issues' misrepresents the cleanup order and mechanism.
findings.location: Why § — live test cleanup description
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'deletes every artifact it created (links then issues)' with 'deletes each created issue via delete_issue (which cascades link removal), skipping delete_issue_link due to a known hang (bug d843).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define a mechanical discriminator for 'always-on guard' tests — either a pytest marker (e.g., `@pytest.mark.external_guard`), a naming convention, or an explicit enumerated list — that the hook can use at runtime to exclude them from the credential-gated executed count, and specify that this discriminator is co-located with the hook's counter logic.
findings.finding: The exclusion rule 'always-on guard tests that carry NO credential skip are excluded from the executed-live count' is a decision rule stated in the hook description but not given a mechanical specification that can be co-located with the counter logic. The only example is `test_external_tier_allows_model_requests`, but other tests also have no Jira credential requirement (e.g., `test_deps_live.py` needs only network, `test_execution_mode_live.py` has its own `@pytest.mark.skipif(not os.environ.get('ANTHROPIC_API_KEY'))` which IS credential-gated). There is no pytest marker, naming convention, or enumerated list that lets the hook distinguish 'always-on guard' from 'credential-gated' at runtime.
findings.location: What, item 2 — 'always-on guard tests excluded from the executed count'; AC item 2
findings.suggested_fix: Introduce a `pytest.mark.external_guard` marker applied to tests like `test_external_tier_allows_model_requests` that never skip on credentials; the hook excludes tests carrying this marker from the 'credential-gated executed' count.

findings.finding: The conftest hook must exclude 'always-on guard' tests from the executed-live count (so they can't mask a real all-skip), but the plan never specifies the MECHANISM by which a passing test is classified as an always-on guard vs a credential-gated live test. For skipped tests the skip-reason regex (cred/acli/live/api.token) provides classification, but a passing test has no skip reason. An executor must either hard-code a list of guard test IDs, introduce a pytest mark, or use a naming convention — none of which is specified.
findings.location: What §2 (fail-on-all-skip canary) / Acceptance Criteria §2
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sentence to §2: 'Tests are classified as credential-gated by the presence of `@pytest.mark.live_credential_required`; the hook counts only marked tests in the executed-live tally and only considers skip-reason matches on marked tests for condition (a). Always-on guards carry no such mark and are invisible to both conditions.'

findings.finding: The 'always-on guard tests EXCLUDED from the executed-live count' decision rule is stated as a processing protocol in prose but no co-located mechanism is specified: the plan names no pytest marker, no nodeid pattern, no hook primitive, and no classification attribute that the hook implementation can use to distinguish always-on guard tests (like `test_external_tier_allows_model_requests`) from credential-gated live tests. The counter accumulation hook primitive (e.g. `pytest_runtest_logreport`, module-level global, plugin attribute) is also unspecified.
findings.location: What item 2: 'always-on guard tests that carry NO credential skip … are EXCLUDED from the executed-live count, so their unconditional execution cannot mask an all-skip live tier'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the classification rule for 'credential-gated live tests' vs 'always-on guard tests' in the conftest hook — e.g., 'a test is credential-gated iff its item nodeid matches the pattern `*live*` OR it has ever produced a skip-reason matching the credential pattern; always-on guard tests are those in a named set / decorated with a `@pytest.mark.external_guard` marker' — so the executor has a deterministic rule to implement without a design choice.
findings.finding: The conftest hook must distinguish 'always-on guard tests' (excluded from the executed-live count) from 'credential-gated live tests' (included), but the plan provides only one example test name and no general classification rule — leaving the executor to choose an appropriate mechanism (e.g., a pytest marker, a naming convention, or skip-reason-based inference).
findings.location: What § 2 (conftest hook) and AC bullet 2
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence to the What § 2 spec: 'A test counts as a credential-gated live test iff its collected skip-reason (if any) matches the `cred|acli|live|api\.token` pattern; tests that EXECUTE without ever producing a matching skip-reason are treated as always-on guards and excluded from the executed-live count. No marker or naming convention is needed — the distinction is derived entirely from the skip-reason signal already being tracked for condition (a).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly state in the plan (What / item 1) that the `permissions:` block for `external-integration.yml` should remain `contents: read` (unchanged from current) — the 'MIRROR EXACTLY' instruction applies only to the three acli steps (cache / download-verify-install / add-to-PATH-and-authenticate), NOT to reconcile-bridge's `contents: write` / `actions: write` grants that serve push-back and self-dispatch operations absent from this workflow.
findings.finding: The plan instructs to 'MIRROR the established reconcile-bridge.yml convention EXACTLY' for the acli steps, but reconcile-bridge.yml also carries `permissions: contents: write` and `actions: write` (required for its push-back and self-dispatch operations that do not apply here). The external-integration workflow currently has the narrower `permissions: contents: read`. The plan never explicitly states which `permissions:` block the modified workflow should declare, leaving an implementer to either (a) over-provision by mirroring reconcile-bridge's elevated grants verbatim, or (b) leave the existing contents: read unchanged — but without a clear directive either is plausible.
findings.location: What / item 1 — 'MIRROR the established reconcile-bridge.yml convention EXACTLY'; .github/workflows/external-integration.yml permissions block
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the What / item 1 section: 'The `permissions:` block stays `contents: read` (unchanged); the "MIRROR EXACTLY" instruction applies only to the three acli steps listed — reconcile-bridge's `contents: write` and `actions: write` grants are NOT mirrored (they serve its push-back and self-dispatch operations, which this workflow does not perform).'

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the hook's behavior when zero external tests are collected under `REBAR_RUN_EXTERNAL=1`: either treat it as a failure (requiring at least one test to have been collected) or as a no-op, and document this in the AC.
findings.finding: The hook's fallback behavior when `REBAR_RUN_EXTERNAL=1` but zero external tests are collected (e.g., pytest is invoked over a path that matches no external tests, or the `external` marker filter is too narrow) is never specified. The plan's two conditions both reference 'skipped' tests, but if there are no collected tests at all there are neither skips nor executions, making both conditions vacuously unsatisfied — the hook's failure logic is undefined for this edge case.
findings.location: What, item 2 — fail-on-all-skip hook; AC item 2

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the pytest hook primitives the fail-on-all-skip implementation must use: name `pytest_runtest_logreport` (to accumulate per-test skip reasons and execution counts into a session-scoped data structure) and `pytest_sessionfinish` or `pytest_terminal_summary` (to evaluate and report the final counts), with the counter stored on a plugin class or a list attached to the pytest `config` object.
findings.finding: The counter/state increment mechanism for the fail-on-all-skip hook is never specified: the plan says the hook 'REPORTS executed-vs-skipped counts' and 'FAILS when ZERO credential-gated live tests EXECUTED', but no pytest hook primitive is named (e.g., `pytest_runtest_logreport`, `pytest_sessionfinish`, `pytest_terminal_summary`), no session-state storage location is described, and no distinction is drawn between how per-test outcomes are accumulated versus the session-level evaluation — leaving the implementation underspecified at the structural level an implementer needs.
findings.location: What, item 2 — conftest hook; tests/external/conftest.py

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend AC#1's config-as-artifact test scope to explicitly assert that the workflow YAML references `vars.ACLI_VERSION` and `vars.ACLI_SHA256` and performs a sha256 integrity check, matching the 'pinned + sha256-checked' requirement in What#1.
findings.finding: AC#1's config-as-artifact test scope does not explicitly require asserting the sha256 integrity check and version-pin variables (ACLI_VERSION, ACLI_SHA256) in the workflow YAML, even though What#1 calls these out as security-critical implementation requirements ('pinned vars.ACLI_VERSION + vars.ACLI_SHA256, sha256-checked').
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC#1 / What section item 1
findings.suggested_fix: Amend AC#1 to read: '…installs acli (using pinned vars.ACLI_VERSION + vars.ACLI_SHA256 with sha256 verification) + symlinks it onto PATH + authenticates (the cache / download-verify-install / add-to-PATH-and-authenticate steps).' The config-as-artifact test should assert that the YAML contains a step referencing `vars.ACLI_SHA256` and a `sha256sum` invocation.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Make the skip-reason matching patterns configurable (e.g. a `CANARY_SKIP_PATTERNS` env var or a pytest ini option) or document them as an explicit extension point in a comment/CLAUDE.md so contributors know to extend the list when adding new live tests.
findings.finding: The skip-reason matching patterns (`cred`/`acli`/`live`/`api.token`) used to classify a skip as 'missing live prerequisite' are hardcoded strings asserted by the author. If a future live test uses a different skip message (e.g. 'missing credentials', 'no token', 'requires live env'), the canary silently stops firing — the all-skip condition is never triggered even though the live tier is still inert. These patterns are an evolving classification rule that needs to be configurable or at minimum documented as an extension point.
findings.location: What §2 (conftest.py hook), AC line 2
findings.suggested_fix: Define the patterns as a module-level constant (e.g. `LIVE_SKIP_PATTERNS = ('cred', 'acli', 'live', 'api.token', 'token')`) in conftest.py with a docstring explaining the extension contract, and add a test asserting that adding a pattern to the list causes the canary to fire for a previously-undetected skip reason.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace skip-reason keyword matching (cred/acli/live/api.token) in the conftest hook with a structural pytest marker (e.g. `pytest.mark.live_cred_gated`) applied to each credential-gated test, so the hook counts marked items that were skipped/executed rather than parsing prose strings.
findings.finding: The conftest hook's 'always-on guard vs credential-gated' classification via prose skip-reason keyword matching (cred/acli/live/api.token) introduces a novel bespoke abstraction that creates fragile string-coupling between hook logic and test prose, where a simpler marker-based approach would be consistent with how the codebase already discriminates test categories.
findings.location: What § 2 (conftest hook) / AC line: 'reason matching, case-insensitively, cred/acli/live/api.token'
findings.suggested_fix: Consolidate the four duplicated `_live_jira_ready()` guards into `tests/external/conftest.py` as a shared helper that also applies `pytest.mark.live_cred_gated` via `request.node.add_marker(...)`, then the session hook counts `item.get_closest_marker('live_cred_gated')` items — no string parsing needed.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether the `on.schedule` trigger should also run the `langfuse-trace` job, or whether it should be scoped (e.g. via job-level `if: github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch'`) to only fire `langfuse-trace` on manual dispatch.
findings.finding: The schedule trigger added to external-integration.yml will also fire the `langfuse-trace` job (which brings up a Docker/Langfuse stack) on every weekly run. The plan names only the `external` job as the recipient of Jira/acli changes, but does not enumerate the `langfuse-trace` job as an unenumerated consumer of the new schedule trigger, nor does it address whether the weekly Langfuse stack spin-up is intended/acceptable.
findings.location: What §1 / .github/workflows/external-integration.yml (langfuse-trace job)
findings.suggested_fix: Add `if: github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch'` to the `langfuse-trace` job so the weekly schedule only runs the `external` job (which is the canary target), or explicitly acknowledge that weekly Langfuse runs are intentional.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Declare the required permission scope of `JIRA_API_TOKEN` in AC item 3: specify that the token should be scoped to the dedicated disposable test project only (project-level create/delete issue + manage links), not an org-wide admin token — and note this in the operator instructions.
findings.finding: The plan mandates a 'dedicated disposable Jira test project' (AC item 3) but never declares or constrains the permission scope of the `JIRA_API_TOKEN` used on the scheduled run. The operator-attested AC asks for the token to be provisioned but places no requirement that it be project-scoped (e.g. to only the disposable test project). An org-wide admin token — the easiest path for an operator — means that a compromised or leaked token has blast radius across the entire Atlassian organization, far beyond the test artifacts the plan creates. The access posture of this boundary-crossing credential is undeclared.
findings.location: AC item 3 (operator-attested) / What §1 ('JIRA_API_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.JIRA_API_TOKEN }}')
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC item 3: 'The JIRA_API_TOKEN must be scoped to the dedicated disposable test project only (minimum: create issue, delete issue, manage issue links); org-wide admin tokens must not be used for this credential.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief design note (inline docstring in conftest.py + a CLAUDE.md / docs entry) documenting the always-on-guard vs credential-gated classification contract, the skip-reason patterns, and the extension protocol for new live tests.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a novel CI canary pattern (pytest session hook that fails when all live tests skip) with no corresponding ADR, CLAUDE.md update, or design-doc note. Contributors who later modify the conftest, add live tests, or adjust skip messages have no documented contract explaining why the hook exists, what the classification rules mean, or how to extend it correctly.
findings.location: What §2 (conftest.py hook), Scope section

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit failure-notification step (e.g. a workflow `if: failure()` step posting to Slack/email/GitHub issue) so scheduled canary failures are operator-visible without manual log inspection.
findings.finding: The plan specifies that the scheduled canary FAILS the session when all live tests skip, but provides no notification/alerting path so operators actually learn about scheduled run failures. A scheduled workflow that fails silently (no Slack/email/PagerDuty hook, no mention of GitHub notification configuration) defeats the canary's purpose: Jira API drift would go undetected until someone manually checks the workflow run history.
findings.location: What §2 (conftest fail-on-all-skip hook) and AC bullet 2; also the overall 'Why' motivation
findings.suggested_fix:
Add a final step to the workflow job:
```yaml
- name: Notify on failure
  if: failure()
  uses: slackapi/slack-github-action@v1
  with:
    payload: '{"text": "external-integration canary FAILED — check for Jira/LLM API drift: ${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.run_id }}"}'
  env:
    SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL: ${{ secrets.SLACK_CANARY_WEBHOOK }}
```
or use GitHub's built-in workflow failure notification by documenting that repository watchers must enable email notifications for Actions failures.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate tests/unit/test_external_isolation.py as a consumer of tests/external/conftest.py that the hook implementation must not break; explicitly call out that the no-op path must use the existing _env_truthy('REBAR_RUN_EXTERNAL') helper (already defined in that conftest) for consistency.
findings.finding: The plan does not enumerate tests/unit/test_external_isolation.py as a consumer that could be affected by the conftest.py session hook. That existing test invokes pytest as a subprocess over tests/external/ with REBAR_RUN_EXTERNAL unset and asserts returncode==0. If the hook implementation uses a conftest-level pytest_sessionfinish (the natural location), it activates for that subprocess session too. The plan's no-op guarantee covers this, but the file is not named as a consumer that implementers must preserve.
findings.location: What §2 / tests/external/conftest.py / tests/unit/test_external_isolation.py
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the plan's Scope or AC: 'the hook must use the existing _env_truthy("REBAR_RUN_EXTERNAL") helper so tests/unit/test_external_isolation.py::test_external_dir_all_skipped_without_opt_in remains green.'

findings.finding: AC3 is labeled '[operator-attested]' and explicitly cannot be verified via repo artifacts or CI — it requires a human operator to configure GitHub repo variables/secrets and provision a Jira project. No deterministic, in-session verification step is specified for the operator to actually confirm the end-to-end setup is correct (e.g., a manual workflow_dispatch run that passes, a specific log line to check). As written, AC3 is satisfied by the operator saying 'done' with no observable artifact.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria §3 ([operator-attested])
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC3: 'Verification: after configuring secrets/variables, the operator triggers a manual `workflow_dispatch` run of `external-integration.yml`, confirms the job exits green (acli authenticated, live Jira tests executed and self-cleaned), and records the GitHub Actions run URL as the attestation artifact.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
